items.title: Jimmy Kimmel defends himself after Donald and Melania Trump call for his firing
items.standfirst: Jimmy Kimmel has responded to Donald and Melania Trump calling for ABC to fire him after a joke he made days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner.
items.content: Jimmy Kimmel has responded to Donald and Melania Trump calling for ABC to fire him after a joke he made days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

items.title: Dinner shooting again put Washington Hilton at center of presidential history
items.standfirst: When shots rang out at the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday, there were echoes of the hotel's storied presidential history.
items.content: When shots rang out at the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents' Dinner on Saturday, there were echoes of the hotel's storied presidential history.

items.title: WHCA dinner shooter charged. And, Charles III to address Congress
items.standfirst: Cole Allen, the man who tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Dinner, is being charged with trying to assassinate President Trump. And, King Charles III is set to address Congress today.
items.content: Cole Allen, the man who tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Dinner, is being charged with trying to assassinate President Trump. And, King Charles III is set to address Congress today.(Image credit: Heather Diehl)

items.title: 'It's bizarre': Californians grapple with revelation that suspected Trump gunman suspect was neighbour
items.standfirst: People living near the man charged with the attempted assassination of Trump say they were shocked to see their neighbour on TV.
items.content: People living near the man charged with the attempted assassination of Trump say they were shocked to see their neighbour on TV.

items.title: Accused media gala gunman charged with attempting to assassinate Trump
items.standfirst: A man accused of trying to open fire on a media gala attended by Donald Trump in Washington this weekend was charged in court Monday with trying to assassinate the Republican president. Cole Allen, 31, faces a sentence of up to life in prison if convicted of attempting to kill Trump during the White House Correspondents Association dinner at a hotel in the capital.
items.content: A man accused of trying to open fire on a media gala attended by Donald Trump in Washington this weekend was charged in court Monday with trying to assassinate the Republican president. Cole Allen, 31, faces a sentence of up to life in prison if convicted of attempting to kill Trump during the White House Correspondents Association dinner at a hotel in the capital.

items.title: King Charles to meet with Trump and deliver address to Congress today
items.standfirst: King Charles III will deliver a rare address to a joint meeting of Congress Tuesday before attending a state dinner at the White House.
items.content: King Charles III will deliver a rare address to a joint meeting of Congress Tuesday before attending a state dinner at the White House.

items.title: UK and US always find ways to come together, King to tell Congress
items.standfirst: King Charles will address the US Congress on Tuesday at a time of diplomatic tensions between London and Washington.
items.content: King Charles will address the US Congress on Tuesday at a time of diplomatic tensions between London and Washington.

items.title: Acting AG Blanche asks court to let White House ballroom construction resume
items.standfirst: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche has asked a federal judge to overturn the judge's own ruling that blocked construction of the White House ballroom, in the wake of the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting.
items.content: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche has asked a federal judge to overturn the judge's own ruling that blocked construction of the White House ballroom, in the wake of the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting.

items.title: Jimmy Kimmel rejects White House criticism over Melania widow joke
items.standfirst: In a parody aired days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner, Kimmel called Melania an "expectant widow".
items.content: In a parody aired days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner, Kimmel called Melania an "expectant widow".

items.title: King Charles expected to promote US-UK unity in speech before Congress
items.standfirst: King Charles III will speak before the US Congress on Tuesday, where he is expected to deliver an address promoting US-UK unity despite widening differences between London and Washington. Charles and Queen Camilla are on a so far studiously apolitical four-day tour of the US.
items.content: King Charles III will speak before the US Congress on Tuesday, where he is expected to deliver an address promoting US-UK unity despite widening differences between London and Washington. Charles and Queen Camilla are on a so far studiously apolitical four-day tour of the US.

items.title: National security expert says Secret Service handled correspondents' dinner shooting well
items.standfirst: The Secret Service is facing questions about its handling of security at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. Professor Philip Bobbitt, director of the Center for National Security at Columbia Law School, joins CBS News with analysis.
items.content: The Secret Service is facing questions about its handling of security at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. Professor Philip Bobbitt, director of the Center for National Security at Columbia Law School, joins CBS News with analysis.

items.title: King Charles, Queen Camilla kick off state visit to U.S.
items.standfirst: Despite Saturday's attack, Britain's King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived in Washington, D.C., on Monday for a four-day trip to the U.S. CBS News royal contributor Amanda Foreman joins CBS News with analysis.
items.content: Despite Saturday's attack, Britain's King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived in Washington, D.C., on Monday for a four-day trip to the U.S. CBS News royal contributor Amanda Foreman joins CBS News with analysis.

items.title: Train collision in Indonesia kills 14, injures dozens
items.standfirst: Indonesia's president ordered an investigation Tuesday after a long-distance train smashed into a stationary commuter train overnight, killing 14 people and injuring dozens. Officials ended a nearly 12-hour rescue effort near Bekasi Timur station, east of the capital Jakarta, which saw crews prying open mangled carriages following the Monday night collision.
items.content: Indonesia's president ordered an investigation Tuesday after a long-distance train smashed into a stationary commuter train overnight, killing 14 people and injuring dozens. Officials ended a nearly 12-hour rescue effort near Bekasi Timur station, east of the capital Jakarta, which saw crews prying open mangled carriages following the Monday night collision.

items.title: BP reports ‘horrifying’ jump in profits as Iran war boosts oil trading; Brent crude hits three-week high – business live
items.standfirst: Energy companies are profiting from Iran war, campaigners warn, after BP’s profits double in first quarter of 2026BP profits more than double as oil and gas prices soar in Iran warAlthough rising energy prices are great news for oil and gas producers, they’re a blow to other businesses, such as housebuilders.This morning, Taylor Wimpey has reported that the cost of building a home is being pushed up, telling shareholders:As a result of rising energy costs, build cost inflation is now expected to be low to mid single digit for 2026, with cost pressure and surcharges starting to come through from our supply chain. Continue reading...
items.content: Energy companies are profiting from Iran war, campaigners warn, after BP’s profits double in first quarter of 2026BP profits more than double as oil and gas prices soar in Iran warAlthough rising energy prices are great news for oil and gas producers, they’re a blow to other businesses, such as housebuilders.This morning, Taylor Wimpey has reported that the cost of building a home is being pushed up, telling shareholders:As a result of rising energy costs, build cost inflation is now expected to be low to mid single digit for 2026, with cost pressure and surcharges starting to come through from our supply chain. items.title: BP profits more than double as oil prices soar in Iran war
items.standfirst: First-quarter profits of $3.2bn prompt outrage from campaigners, who say figures come at expense of consumersBusiness live – latest updatesBP has provoked outrage by revealing its profits more than doubled in the first quarter of this year after its oil traders reaped the benefit of the war in Iran.The energy company capitalised on a surge in global oil market prices to report better than expected profits of $3.2bn (£2.4bn) for the first quarter, more than double the $1.38bn it made in the same period last year. Continue reading...

items.title: U.S. appears cool on Iran proposal to end war and reopen Hormuz without a nuclear deal
items.standfirst: The United States showed little immediate enthusiasm for a new Iranian proposal that would end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz without resolving the impasse over the Islamic Republic's nuclear program.
items.content: The United States showed little immediate enthusiasm for a new Iranian proposal that would end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz without resolving the impasse over the Islamic Republic's nuclear program.

items.title: Iran offers to reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts its blockade and the war ends
items.standfirst: Iran offered to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts its blockade on the country and ends the war in a proposal that would postpone discussions on the Islamic Republic's nuclear program, two regional officials said Monday. US President Donald Trump seems unlikely to accept the offer, which was passed to the Americans by Pakistan and would leave unresolved the disagreements that led the US and Israel to go to war on Feb. 28.
items.content: Iran offered to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts its blockade on the country and ends the war in a proposal that would postpone discussions on the Islamic Republic's nuclear program, two regional officials said Monday. US President Donald Trump seems unlikely to accept the offer, which was passed to the Americans by Pakistan and would leave unresolved the disagreements that led the US and Israel to go to war on Feb. 28.

clusters.title: Tuareg rebels and jihadists in control of key Mali town after offensive
clusters.neutral_headline: Mali’s militant attacks expose limits of Putin’s power in Africa

items.title: Tuareg rebels and jihadists in control of key Mali town after offensive
items.standfirst: Tuareg separatists and their jihadist allies in Mali have seized control of the key northern town of Kidal after coordinated attacks on strategic junta positions, an ally of the local governor and local sources said Monday. Two days of fierce fighting between the army and jihadists allied with the Tuaregs have plunged the junta-led west African country into uncertainty over the military government's future. Defence Minister Sadio Camara has been killed and military ruler General Assimi Goita, who led the coup in 2020 that brought the junta to power, has not been seen or spoken publicly since fighting began at the weekend.
items.content: Tuareg separatists and their jihadist allies in Mali have seized control of the key northern town of Kidal after coordinated attacks on strategic junta positions, an ally of the local governor and local sources said Monday. Two days of fierce fighting between the army and jihadists allied with the Tuaregs have plunged the junta-led west African country into uncertainty over the military government's future. Defence Minister Sadio Camara has been killed and military ruler General Assimi Goita, who led the coup in 2020 that brought the junta to power, has not been seen or spoken publicly since fighting began at the weekend.

items.title: Mali’s militant attacks expose limits of Putin’s power in Africa
items.standfirst: Russian backing for the ruling junta has not stopped rebel fighters striking significant blows in recent daysMali in turmoil after insurgents seize towns and kill defence ministerWhen Assimi Goïta, the leader of Mali’s military junta, sat down with the Russian president, Vladimir Putin, in the Kremlin last summer, it symbolised Moscow’s commanding sway over Mali at the expense of the west.As the two men spoke, roughly 3,500 miles to the south, about 2,000 Russian troops were propping up the regime in the landlocked desert country, as part of Moscow’s broader push for influence across the Sahel region. Continue reading...
items.content: Russian backing for the ruling junta has not stopped rebel fighters striking significant blows in recent daysMali in turmoil after insurgents seize towns and kill defence ministerWhen Assimi Goïta, the leader of Mali’s military junta, sat down with the Russian president, Vladimir Putin, in the Kremlin last summer, it symbolised Moscow’s commanding sway over Mali at the expense of the west.As the two men spoke, roughly 3,500 miles to the south, about 2,000 Russian troops were propping up the regime in the landlocked desert country, as part of Moscow’s broader push for influence across the Sahel region. Continue reading...

items.title: Mali in turmoil after insurgents seize towns and kill defence minister
items.standfirst: Military intelligence chief reportedly also killed in sweeping attacks by jihadists and separatist rebelsMali’s militant attacks expose limits of Putin’s power in AfricaMali has been left reeling from sweeping attacks by jihadists and separatist rebels who seized several towns and military bases and killed the defence minister and military intelligence chief.The weekend assault on the west African state’s security architecture was coordinated by al-Qaida-affiliated Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) and the separatist Tuareg-led movement Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) – former foes with distinct agendas. Continue reading...
items.content: Military intelligence chief reportedly also killed in sweeping attacks by jihadists and separatist rebelsMali’s militant attacks expose limits of Putin’s power in AfricaMali has been left reeling from sweeping attacks by jihadists and separatist rebels who seized several towns and military bases and killed the defence minister and military intelligence chief.The weekend assault on the west African state’s security architecture was coordinated by al-Qaida-affiliated Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) and the separatist Tuareg-led movement Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) – former foes with distinct agendas. clusters.title: Gunmen kill at least 29 in northeast Nigeria after targeting young people at football pitch
clusters.neutral_headline: Gunmen kill at least 29 at football pitch in north-east Nigeria, governor says

items.title: Gunmen kill at least 29 in northeast Nigeria after targeting young people at football pitch
items.standfirst: Gunmen killed at least 29 people in an hours-long attack in Nigeria's northeastern Adamawa state, local authorities said, burning homes and places of worship and opening fire on people watching a football match. Nigeria continues to struggle with armed attacks by jihadists and armed criminal gangs.
items.content: Gunmen killed at least 29 people in an hours-long attack in Nigeria's northeastern Adamawa state, local authorities said, burning homes and places of worship and opening fire on people watching a football match. Nigeria continues to struggle with armed attacks by jihadists and armed criminal gangs.

items.title: Gunmen kill at least 29 at football pitch in north-east Nigeria, governor says
items.standfirst: Attack in Adamawa state continues wave of violence across the country, including armed raid on orphanage in KogiGunmen have killed at least 29 people in north-east Nigeria, a state governor said on Monday, with local people saying the attackers targeted young people gathered at a football pitch, the latest bout of deadly unrest in Africa’s most populous nation.The attack on Sunday occurred in Adamawa state, which borders Cameroon, and is a hotspot for violence by jihadists and criminal gangs. Communal violence over conflict for land is also rife in the state. Continue reading...
items.content: Attack in Adamawa state continues wave of violence across the country, including armed raid on orphanage in KogiGunmen have killed at least 29 people in north-east Nigeria, a state governor said on Monday, with local people saying the attackers targeted young people gathered at a football pitch, the latest bout of deadly unrest in Africa’s most populous nation.The attack on Sunday occurred in Adamawa state, which borders Cameroon, and is a hotspot for violence by jihadists and criminal gangs. Communal violence over conflict for land is also rife in the state. Continue reading...

items.title: Tuareg rebels in control of Mali's Kidal
items.standfirst: In tonight's edition: Two days after a wave of coordinated attacks across the country, Mali remains on edge. Also, 42 people are killed in Chad after a clash between two families over a water point escalated into a cycle of reprisals. Plus Kenya's Sabastian Sawe smashes the two-hour barrier at the London Marathon.
items.content: In tonight's edition: Two days after a wave of coordinated attacks across the country, Mali remains on edge. Also, 42 people are killed in Chad after a clash between two families over a water point escalated into a cycle of reprisals. Plus Kenya's Sabastian Sawe smashes the two-hour barrier at the London Marathon.

clusters.title: Carney announces Canada’s first sovereign wealth fund
clusters.neutral_headline: Canada's Carney launches a sovereign wealth fund. What is it
bullet_summary: Reported by Reuters Top News, BBC World

items.title: Carney announces Canada’s first sovereign wealth fund
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Mark Carney says the new Canada Strong Fund will be Canada’s first sovereign wealth fund, with an initial federal contribution of $25 billion. It is intended to invest alongside the private sector in major projects and companies in areas such as energy, infrastructure, mining, agriculture and technology. [cbc.ca]
items.content: Prime Minister Mark Carney says the new Canada Strong Fund will be Canada’s first sovereign wealth fund, with an initial federal contribution of $25 billion. It is intended to invest alongside the private sector in major projects and companies in areas such as energy, infrastructure, mining, agriculture and technology. [cbc.ca]

items.title: Canada's Carney launches a sovereign wealth fund. What is it?
items.standfirst: The fund - which Canadians can invest in directly - will help pay for major infrastructure projects in the country, the prime minister says.

items.title: Bomb blast on Colombia highway leaves 21 dead amid pre-election violence
items.standfirst: Cocaine-trafficking rebels blamed for worst attack on civilians in decades, which also left 56 people injuredThe death toll in a Colombian highway bombing blamed on cocaine-trafficking rebels has risen to 21, the government said on Monday, in the country’s worst attack on civilians in decades and just ahead of elections.The attack on Saturday left 56 injured and buses and vans mangled on the Pan-American Highway, in the restive south-western Cauca department. Continue reading...
